{"type":"message","id":"bf6e63c4","parentId":"43cb6a19","timestamp":"2026-05-06T15:46:15.766Z","message":{"role":"toolResult","toolCallId":"ollama_call_e0699a82-4e60-48c2-8bf7-96efd4a90c4f","toolName":"read","content":[{"type":"text","text":"---\nname: llm-wiki\nversion: \"1.0.0\"\ndescription: \"OpenClaw LLM-Wiki core tools: compile sessions, BM25 search, budget-aware reads, AI exports\"\nauthor: \"OpenClaw\"\ntags: [wiki, knowledge, bm25, sessions, compile]\ncategory: \"knowledge\"\n---\n\n# OpenClaw LLM-Wiki Skill\n\nCompile OpenClaw sessions into a structured wiki with BM25 search, budget-aware reads, and AI-consumable exports.\n\n## Commands\n\n| Command | Params | Description |\n|---------|--------|-------------|\n| `compile` | `--since <date>` `--limit <N>` | Compile recent sessions → wiki pages |\n| `search` | `q=<query>` `scope=<all\\|sessions\\|entities\\|concepts>` `limit=<N>` | BM25 search over wiki |\n| `read` | `slug=<slug>` `budget=<tokens>` | Budget-aware page viewport |\n| `export` | `format=<llms-txt\\|jsonld>` | Generate AI-consumable exports |\n\n---\n\n## Implementation\n\n```python\nimport sqlite3\nimport json\nimport re\nimport os\nfrom datetime import datetime\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nWIKI_DIR = Path(\"/home/poomk/.openclaw/workspace/wiki\")\nINDEX_DB = WIKI_DIR.parent / \"wiki-index\" / \"index.db\"\nSESSIONS_DIR = WIKI_DIR / \"sessions\"\nENTITIES_DIR = WIKI_DIR / \"entities\"\nCONCEPTS_DIR = WIKI_DIR / \"concepts\"\n\ndef get_db():\n    \"\"\"Get SQLite connection with FTS5 BM25 support.\"\"\"\n    conn = sqlite3.connect(str(INDEX_DB))\n    conn.row_factory = sqlite3.Row\n    return conn\n\ndef parse_wikilinks(content):\n    \"\"\"Extract [[wikilinks]] from markdown content.\"\"\"\n    pattern = r'\\[\\[([^\\]|]+)(?:\\|([^\\]]+))?\\]\\]'\n    links = []\n    for match in re.finditer(pattern, content):\n        slug = match.group(1).strip()\n        display = match.group(2) if match.group(2) else slug\n        links.append({\"slug\": slug, \"display\": display})\n    return links\n\ndef estimate_tokens(text):\n    \"\"\"Rough token estimate: 1 token ≈ 4 chars.\"\"\"\n    return len(text) // 4\n\n# ============================================================================\n# WIKI COMPILE — Session → Markdown Pages\n# ============================================================================\n\ndef wiki_compile(since=None, limit=5):\n    \"\"\"\n    Compile recent OpenClaw sessions into wiki pages.\n    \n    Args:\n        since: ISO date string (e.g., \"2026-05-01\") or \"7d\" for last 7 days\n        limit: Max sessions to compile\n    \n    Returns:\n        List of compiled page slugs\n    \"\"\"\n    import subprocess\n    \n    # Fetch recent sessions via OpenClaw CLI\n    cmd = [\"openclaw\", \"sessions\", \"--json\"]\n    if since:\n        if since.endswith(\"d\"):\n            days = int(since[:-1])\n            # Calculate minutes\n            since = str(days * 24 * 60)\n        cmd.extend([\"--active\", str(since)])\n    \n    result = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True, cwd=str(WIKI_DIR.parent))\n    sessions_data = json.loads(result.stdout)\n    sessions = sessions_data.get(\"sessions\", [])[:limit]\n    \n    compiled = []\n    for session in sessions:\n        slug = _compile_session(session)\n        if slug:\n            compiled.append(slug)\n    \n    return compiled\n\ndef _compile_session(session):\n    \"\"\"Compile a single session into a wiki page.\"\"\"\n    session_id = session.get(\"sessionId\", \"unknown\")\n    session_file = session.get(\"sessionFile\", \"\")\n    \n    # Read session transcript\n    if not os.path.exists(session_file):\n        return None\n    \n    with open(session_file, 'r') as f:\n        transcript = f.read()\n    \n    # Extract key info (simplified extraction)\n    summary = _extract_summary(transcript)\n    decisions = _extract_decisions(transcript)\n    errors_fixes = _extract_errors(transcript)\n    entities = _extract_entities(transcript)\n    concepts = _extract_concepts(transcript)\n    \n    # Generate slug from session date + key topic\n    updated_at = session.get(\"updatedAt\", 0)\n    date_str = datetime.fromtimestamp(updated_at / 1000).strftime(\"%Y-%m-%d\")\n    topic = entities[0] if entities else \"session\"\n    slug = f\"{date_str}-{topic.lower().replace(' ', '-')}\"\n    \n    # Build markdown content\n    content = _build_page_content(summary, decisions, errors_fixes, entities, concepts, session_id, date_str)\n    \n    # Write page\n    page_path = SESSIONS_DIR / f\"{slug}.md\"\n    page_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n    \n    with open(page_path, 'w') as f:\n        f.write(content)\n    \n    # Parse wikilinks and update index\n    links = parse_wikilinks(content)\n    _update_index(slug, \"session\", content, session_id, date_str, entities, concepts, links)\n    \n    # Git commit\n    _git_commit(f\"wiki: compile {session_id} → {slug}\", [str(page_path)])\n    \n    return slug\n\ndef _extract_summary(transcript):\n    \"\"\"Extract session summary from transcript.\"\"\"\n    # Simplified: first 500 chars as summary\n    lines = transcript.split('\\n')\n    summary_lines = []\n    for line in lines[:50]:\n        if line.strip() and not line.startswith('{'):\n            summary_lines.append(line)\n    return '\\n'.join(summary_lines)[:500]\n\ndef _extract_decisions(transcript):\n    \"\"\"Extract key decisions from transcript.\"\"\"\n    decisions = []\n    for line in transcript.split('\\n'):\n        if 'decided' in line.lower() or 'decision' in line.lower():\n            decisions.append(line.strip())\n    return decisions[:5]\n\ndef _extract_errors(transcript):\n    \"\"\"Extract errors and fixes from transcript.\"\"\"\n    errors = []\n    for line in transcript.split('\\n'):\n        if 'error' in line.lower() or 'fix' in line.lower() or 'bug' in line.lower():\n            errors.append(line.strip())\n    return errors[:5]\n\ndef _extract_entities(transcript):\n    \"\"\"Extract entity mentions from transcript.\"\"\"\n    # Simplified: look for project names, tool names\n    entities = []\n    known_entities = ['openclaw', 'threejs', 'vue', 'github', 'telegram', 'ollama']\n    for entity in known_entities:\n        if entity.lower() in transcript.lower():\n            entities.append(entity)\n    return list(set(entities))[:5]\n\ndef _extract_concepts(transcript):\n    \"\"\"Extract abstract concepts from transcript.\"\"\"\n    concepts = []\n    known_concepts = ['context-management', 'debugging', 'ci-pipeline', 'game-loop']\n    for concept in known_concepts:\n        if concept.lower() in transcript.lower():\n            concepts.append(concept)\n    return list(set(concepts))[:5]\n\ndef _build_page_content(summary, decisions, errors_fixes, entities, concepts, session_id, date_str):\n    \"\"\"Build markdown page with YAML frontmatter.\"\"\"\n    entities_list = ', '.join(entities) if entities else 'none'\n    concepts_list = ', '.join(concepts) if concepts else 'none'\n    \n    # Build wikilinks\n    entity_links = ' '.join([f\"[[{e}]]\" for e in entities])\n    concept_links = ' '.join([f\"[[{c}]]\" for c in concepts])\n    \n    frontmatter = f\"\"\"---\nslug: {date_str}-session\ntype: session\ndate: {date_str}\nsession_id: {session_id}\nentities: [{entities_list}]\nconcepts: [{concepts_list}]\nconfidence: 0.75\nlifecycle: draft\n---\n\n# Session {date_str}\n\n## Summary\n\n{summary}\n\n## Key Decisions\n\n\"\"\"\n    \n    if decisions:\n        for d in decisions:\n            frontmatter += f\"- {d}\\n\"\n    else:\n        frontmatter += \"- No explicit decisions recorded\\n\"\n    \n    frontmatter += \"\\n## Errors & Fixes\\n\\n\"\n    \n    if errors_fixes:\n        for e in errors_fixes:\n            frontmatter += f\"- {e}\\n\"\n    else:\n        frontmatter += \"- No errors recorded\\n\"\n    \n    frontmatter += f\"\\n## Related\\n\\n\"\n    if entity_links:\n        frontmatter += f\"**Entities:** {entity_links}\\n\"\n    if concept_links:\n        frontmatter += f\"**Concepts:** {concept_links}\\n\"\n    \n    return frontmatter\n\n# ============================================================================\n# WIKI SEARCH — BM25 over SQLite FTS5\n# ============================================================================\n\ndef wiki_search(query, scope=\"all\", limit=10):\n    \"\"\"\n    BM25 search over wiki pages.\n    \n    Args:\n        query: Search query string\n        scope: Filter by type (sessions, entities, concepts, all)\n        limit: Max results to return\n    \n    Returns:\n        List of {slug, score, snippet, type}\n    \"\"\"\n    conn = get_db()\n    cursor = conn.cursor()\n    \n    # Build FTS5 query\n    if scope != \"all\":\n        query_str = f\"{query} AND type={scope}\"\n    else:\n        query_str = query\n    \n    sql = \"\"\"\n        SELECT slug, title, type, bm25(wiki_pages) AS score\n        FROM wiki_pages\n        WHERE wiki_pages MATCH ?\n        ORDER BY score DESC\n        LIMIT ?\n    \"\"\"\n    \n    cursor.execute(sql, (query_str, limit))\n    rows = cursor.fetchall()\n    \n    results = []\n    for row in rows:\n        # Get snippet from body\n        snippet = _get_snippet(row['slug'], query)\n        results.append({\n            \"slug\": row['slug'],\n            \"score\": round(-row['score'], 4),  # BM25 returns negative scores\n            \"snippet\": snippet,\n            \"type\": row['type']\n        })\n    \n    conn.close()\n    return results\n\ndef _get_snippet(slug, query, max_chars=150):\n    \"\"\"Get a snippet from the page body around query terms.\"\"\"\n    conn = get_db()\n    cursor = conn.cursor()\n    cursor.execute(\"SELECT body FROM wiki_pages WHERE slug = ?\", (slug,))\n    row = cursor.fetchone()\n    conn.close()\n    \n    if not row:\n        return \"\"\n    \n    body = row['body']\n    # Find first occurrence of query term\n    body_lower = body.lower()\n    query_lower = query.lower()\n    \n    idx = body_lower.find(query_lower)\n    if idx == -1:\n        idx = 0\n    \n    start = max(0, idx - 50)\n    end = min(len(body), idx + max_chars)\n    \n    snippet = body[start:end]\n    if start > 0:\n        snippet = \"...\" + snippet\n    if end < len(body):\n        snippet = snippet + \"...\"\n    \n    return snippet.replace('\\n', ' ').strip()\n\n# ============================================================================\n# WIKI READ — Budget-Aware Page Viewport\n# ============================================================================\n\ndef wiki_read(slug, budget=2000):\n    \"\"\"\n    Read a wiki page with token budget enforcement.\n    \n    Args:\n        slug: Page slug to read\n        budget: Max tokens to return (default 2000 ≈ 8000 chars)\n    \n    Returns:\n        Dict with frontmatter, summary, sections, links, budget_info\n    \"\"\"\n    # Determine page type and path\n    for page_type in ['sessions', 'entities', 'concepts', 'syntheses', 'questions', 'comparisons', 'sources']:\n        page_path = WIKI_DIR / page_type / f\"{slug}.md\"\n        if page_path.exists():\n            break\n    else:\n        return {\"error\": f\"Page not found: {slug}\"}\n    \n    with open(page_path, 'r') as f:\n        content = f.read()\n    \n    # Parse frontmatter\n    frontmatter, body = _parse_frontmatter(content)\n    \n    # Enforce token budget\n    max_chars = budget * 4  # ~4 chars per token\n    total_chars = len(body)\n    \n    # Extract sections\n    sections = _extract_sections(body)\n    \n    # Build viewport within budget\n    viewport_parts = []\n    chars_used = 0\n    \n    # Always include summary\n    if sections.get('summary'):\n        viewport_parts.append(f\"## Summary\\n\\n{sections['summary']}\\n\")\n        chars_used += len(sections['summary']) + 15\n    \n    # Add sections until budget\n    truncated_sections = []\n    for section_name, section_content in sections.items():\n        if section_name == 'summary':\n            continue\n        if chars_used + len(section_content) + 50 > max_chars:\n            truncated_sections.append(section_name)\n            # Add header only\n            viewport_parts.append(f\"## {section_name}\\n\\n[...truncated...]\\n\")\n            chars_used += 50\n            break\n        viewport_parts.append(f\"## {section_name}\\n\\n{section_content}\\n\")\n        chars_used += len(section_content) + 50\n    \n    # Get links\n    outgoing = parse_wikilinks(body)\n    incoming = _get_backlinks(slug)\n    \n    tokens_used = estimate_tokens('\\n'.join(viewport_parts))\n    \n    return {\n        \"slug\": slug,\n        \"type\": page_type,\n        \"frontmatter\": frontmatter,\n\n⚠️ [... middle content omitted — showing head and tail ...]\n\n    cursor = conn.cursor()\n    \n    # Parse frontmatter for title\n    frontmatter, body = _parse_frontmatter(content)\n    title = frontmatter.get('slug', slug)\n    \n    # Upsert into FTS5\n    cursor.execute(\"\"\"\n        INSERT OR REPLACE INTO wiki_pages (slug, title, body, type, entities, concepts)\n        VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\n    \"\"\", (slug, title, body, page_type, ','.join(entities), ','.join(concepts)))\n    \n    # Upsert metadata\n    cursor.execute(\"\"\"\n        INSERT OR REPLACE INTO wiki_meta (slug, type, date, session_id, entities, concepts, last_compiled)\n        VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\n    \"\"\", (slug, page_type, date_str, session_id, json.dumps(entities), json.dumps(concepts), datetime.now().isoformat()))\n    \n    # Clear old links for this page\n    cursor.execute(\"DELETE FROM wiki_links WHERE from_slug = ?\", (slug,))\n    \n    # Insert new links\n    for link in links:\n        cursor.execute(\"\"\"\n            INSERT OR IGNORE INTO wiki_links (from_slug, to_slug, context)\n            VALUES (?, ?, ?)\n        \"\"\", (slug, link['slug'], link['display']))\n    \n    conn.commit()\n    conn.close()\n\ndef _git_commit(message, files):\n    \"\"\"Git commit wiki changes.\"\"\"\n    import subprocess\n    \n    try:\n        subprocess.run(\n            [\"git\", \"add\"] + files,\n            cwd=str(WIKI_DIR.parent),\n            capture_output=True,\n            check=True\n        )\n        subprocess.run(\n            [\"git\", \"commit\", \"-m\", message, \"--author=OpenClaw Wiki <wiki@openclaw.ai>\"],\n            cwd=str(WIKI_DIR.parent),\n            capture_output=True,\n            check=True\n        )\n    except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:\n        print(f\"Git commit failed: {e}\")\n\n# ============================================================================\n# CLI ENTRY POINT\n# ============================================================================\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    import sys\n    \n    if len(sys.argv) < 2:\n        print(\"Usage: wiki-skill.py <command> [args]\")\n        print(\"Commands: compile, search, read, export\")\n        sys.exit(1)\n    \n    command = sys.argv[1]\n    \n    if command == \"compile\":\n        since = sys.argv[2] if len(sys.argv) > 2 else None\n        limit = int(sys.argv[3]) if len(sys.argv) > 3 else 5\n        result = wiki_compile(since, limit)\n        print(f\"Compiled {len(result)} sessions: {result}\")\n    \n    elif command == \"search\":\n        query = sys.argv[2] if len(sys.argv) > 2 else \"\"\n        scope = sys.argv[3] if len(sys.argv) > 3 else \"all\"\n        limit = int(sys.argv[4]) if len(sys.argv) > 4 else 10\n        results = wiki_search(query, scope, limit)\n        for r in results:\n            print(f\"[{r['score']:.4f}] {r['type']}/{r['slug']}: {r['snippet']}\")\n    \n    elif command == \"read\":\n        slug = sys.argv[2] if len(sys.argv) > 2 else \"\"\n        budget = int(sys.argv[3]) if len(sys.argv) > 3 else 2000\n        result = wiki_read(slug, budget)\n        if \"error\" in result:\n            print(result[\"error\"])\n        else:\n            print(result[\"viewport\"])\n            print(f\"\\n--- Links: {len(result['outgoing_links'])} outgoing, {len(result['incoming_links'])} incoming ---\")\n    \n    elif command == \"export\":\n        format = sys.argv[2] if len(sys.argv) > 2 else \"llms-txt\"\n        path = wiki_export(format)\n        print(f\"Exported to: {path}\")\n    \n    else:\n        print(f\"Unknown command: {command}\")\n        sys.exit(1)\n```\n\n---\n\n## Usage Examples\n\n```bash\n# Compile last 5 sessions\npython wiki-skill.py compile\n\n# Compile sessions from last 7 days\npython wiki-skill.py compile 7d 10\n\n# Search for \"context budget\"\npython wiki-skill.py search \"context budget\" all 5\n\n# Read a page with 2000 token budget\npython wiki-skill.py read boss-spawn-bug 2000\n\n# Export llms.txt\npython wiki-skill.py export llms-txt\n\n# Export JSON-LD graph\npython wiki-skill.py export jsonld\n```\n\n---\n\n*Follows ARCHITECTURE.md sections 5-7. Uses SQLite FTS5 for BM25, git for audit trail, token-budgeted viewports.*\n[... 7101 more characters truncated]"}],"isError":false,"timestamp":1778082375766}}

uild.bat](/Pratiyush/llm-wiki/blob/master/build.bat)\\n\\n \\n \\n\\n[build.sh](/Pratiyush/llm-wiki/blob/master/build.sh)\\n\\n[build.sh](/Pratiyush/llm-wiki/blob/master/build.sh)\\n\\n \\n \\n\\n[docker-compose.yml](/Pratiyush/llm-wiki/blob/master/docker-compose.yml)\\n\\n[docker-compose.yml](/Pratiyush/llm-wiki/blob/master/docker-compose.yml)\\n\\n \\n \\n\\n[flake.nix](/Pratiyush/llm-wiki/blob/master/flake.nix)\\n\\n[flake.nix](/Pratiyush/llm-wiki/blob/master/flake.nix)\\n\\n \\n \\n\\n[lychee.toml](/Pratiyush/llm-wiki/blob/master/lychee.toml)\\n\\n[lychee.toml](/Pratiyush/llm-wiki/blob/master/lychee.toml)\\n\\n \\n \\n\\n[package-lock.json](/Pratiyush/llm-wiki/blob/master/package-lock.json)\\n\\n[package-lock.json](/Pratiyush/llm-wiki/blob/master/package-lock.json)\\n\\n \\n \\n\\n[package.json](/Pratiyush/llm-wiki/blob/master/\n\n⚠️ bottom of every session\\n\\n- Activity heatmap on the home page\\n\\n- Model info cards with structured schema (provider, pricing, benchmarks)\\n\\n- Auto-generated vs-comparison pages between AI models\\n\\n- Append-only changelog timeline with pricing sparkline\\n\\n- Project topic chips (GitHub-style tags on project cards)\\n\\n- Agent labels (colored badges: Claude/Codex/Copilot/Cursor/Gemini)\\n\\n- Recently-updated card on the home page\\n\\n- Dataview-style structured queries in the command palette\\n\\n- Hover-to-preview wikilinks\\n\\n- Deep-link icons next to every heading\\n\\n- Mobile-responsive + print-friendly\\n\\n### AI-consumable (v0.4)\\n#ai-consumable-v04\\n\\nEvery HTML page has sibling machine-readable files at the same URL:\\n\\n- .html — human HTML with schema.org microdata\\n\\n- .txt — plain text version (no HTML tags)\\n\\n- .json — structured metadata + body\\n\\nSite-level AI-agent entry points:\\n\\nFile\\nWhat\\n\\n[/llms.txt](https://llmstxt.org)\\nShort index per [llmstxt.org spec](https://llmstxt.org)\\n\\n/llms-full.txt\\nFlattened plain-text dump (~5 MB cap) — paste into any LLM's context\\n\\n/graph.jsonld\\nSchema.org JSON-LD entity/concept/source graph\\n\\n/sitemap.xml\\nStandard sitemap with lastmod\\n\\n/rss.xml\\nRSS 2.0 feed of newest sessions\\n\\n/robots.txt\\nAI-friendly robots with llms.txt reference\\n\\n/ai-readme.md\\nAI-specific navigation instructions\\n\\n/manifest.json\\nBuild manifest with SHA-256 hashes + perf budget\\n\\nEvery page also includes an <!-- llmwiki:metadata --> HTML comment that AI agents can parse without fetching the separate .json sibling.\\n\\n#### Recipe — query graph.jsonld from your terminal\\n#recipe--query-graphjsonld-from-your-terminal\\n\\nThe JSON-LD graph isn't just for crawlers — you can ask quick questions about your wiki without leaving the shell. Example: print every session as a tree, grouped by project:\\n\\npython3 examples/scripts/tree_from_graph.py\\n\\nOutput:\\n\\n📚 8 sessions across 3 projects\\n (site/graph.jsonld v1.3.0)\\n\\nllmwiki/\\n├── demo-blog-engine/ (4 sessions)\\n│ ├── 2026-03-12 scaffolding-the-rust-blog-engine\\n│ ├── 2026-03-18 adding-syntax-highlighting\\n│ ├── 2026-03-25 rss-feed-and-sitemap\\n│ └── 2026-04-01 dark-mode-toggle\\n├── demo-ml-pipeline/ (2 sessions)\\n│ ├── 2026-01-20 training-data-pipeline\\n│ └── 2026-02-02 model-training-loop\\n└── demo-todo-api/ (2 sessions)\\n ├── 2026-02-08 fastapi-project-bootstrap\\n └── 2026-02-15 adding-oauth-login\\n\\nThe full script is stdlib-only at [examples/scripts/tree_from_graph.py](/Pratiyush/llm-wiki/blob/master/examples/scripts/tree_from_graph.py). Same recipe pattern works for any aggregation question — count sessions per model, find the largest project by token usage, list every entity that appears in 3+ sessions, etc. The graph is yours to slice.\\n\\n### Quality & governance (v1.0)\\n#quality--governance-v10\\n\\n- 4-factor confidence scoring — source count, source quality, recency, cross-references; with Ebbinghaus-inspired decay per content-type\\n\\n- 5-state lifecycle machine — draft → reviewed → verified → stale → archived with 90-day auto-stale\\n\\n- 16 lint rules — 8 structural (frontmatter, link integrity, orphans, freshness, duplicates, index sync…) + 3 LLM-powered (contradictions, claim verification, summary accuracy) + stale_candidates (#51) + tags_topics_convention (#302) + stale_reference_detection (#303) + frontmatter_count_consistency (#378) + tools_consistency (#378)\\n\\n- Auto Dream — MEMORY.md consolidation after 24h + 5 sessions: resolve relative dates, prune outdated, 200-line cap\\n\\n- 9 navigation files — CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, MEMORY.md, SOUL.md, CRITICAL_FACTS.md, hints.md, hot.md + per-project hot caches\\n\\n### Obsidian-native experience (v1.0)\\n#obsidian-native-experience-v10\\n\\n- link-obsidian CLI — symlinks the whole project into an Obsidia\\n<<<END_EXTERNAL_UNTRUSTED_CONTENT id=\\\"9a0198fbeae11b92\\\">>>\"\n}[... 5559 more characters truncated]"}],"details":{"persistedDetailsTruncated":true,"originalDetailsBytesAtLeast":8193,"originalDetailKeys":["url","finalUrl","status","contentType","title","extractMode","extractor","externalContent","truncated","length","rawLength","wrappedLength","fetchedAt","tookMs","text","warning"],"status":200,"truncated":true},"isError":false,"timestamp":1778048428316}}

date\\n\\n \\n\\n/Labhund/llm-wiki/commits/master/\\n\\n[docs](/Labhund/llm-wiki/tree/master/docs)\\n\\n[docs](/Labhund/llm-wiki/tree/master/docs)\\n\\n \\n \\n\\n[skills/setup](/Labhund/llm-wiki/tree/master/skills/setup)\\n\\n[skills/setup](/Labhund/llm-wiki/tree/master/skills/setup)\\n\\n \\n \\n\\n[src/llm_wiki](/Labhund/llm-wiki/tree/master/src/llm_wiki)\\n\\n[src/llm_wiki](/Labhund/llm-wiki/tree/master/src/llm_wiki)\\n\\n \\n \\n\\n[tests](/Labhund/llm-wiki/tree/master/tests)\\n\\n[tests](/Labhund/llm-wiki/tree/master/tests)\\n\\n \\n \\n\\n[wiki](/Labhund/llm-wiki/tree/master/wiki)\\n\\n[wiki](/Labhund/llm-wiki/tree/master/wiki)\\n\\n \\n \\n\\n[.gitignore](/Labhund/llm-wiki/blob/master/.gitignore)\\n\\n[.gitignore](/Labhund/llm-wiki/blob/master/.gitignore)\\n\\n \\n \\n\\n[LICENSE](/Labhund/llm-wiki/blob/master/LICENSE)\\n\\n[LICENSE](/Labhund/llm-wiki/blob/master/LICENSE)\\n\\n \\n \\n\\n[PHILOSOPHY.md](/Labhund/llm-wiki/blob/master/PHILOSOPHY.md)\\n\\n[PHILOSOPHY.md](/Labhund/llm-wiki/blob/master/PHILOSOPHY.md)\\n\\n \\n \\n\\n[README.md](/Labhund/llm-wiki/blob/master/README.md)\\n\\n[README.md](/Labhund/llm-wiki/blob/master/README.md)\\n\\n \\n \\n\\n[TODO.md](/Labhund/llm-wiki/blob/master/TODO.md)\\n\\n[TODO.md](/Labhund/llm-wiki/blob/master/TODO.md)\\n\\n \\n \\n\\n[pyproject.toml](/Labhund/llm-wiki/blob/master/pyproject.toml)\\n\\n[pyproject.toml](/Labhund/llm-wiki/blob/master/pyproject.toml)\\n\\n \\n \\n\\nView all files\\n\\n## Repository files navigation\\n\\n- [README](#)\\n- [MIT license](#)\\n\\n# LLM Wiki\\n#llm-wiki\\n\\nThis project is deprecated. Development has moved to [lacuna-wiki](https://github.com/Labhund/lacuna-wiki), a cleaner rewrite with a simpler single-tool MCP surface, structured agent skills, and a compounding knowledge graph. Use that instead.\\n\\nAn agent-first knowledge base: plain markdown with wikilinks, a daemon that keeps it indexed and honest, and an MCP server that lets agents navigate it the way a researcher reads Wikipedia.\\n\\nThe LLM writes and maintains the wiki; the human reads and asks questions.\\n— [Andrej Karpathy](https://x.com/karpathy/status/2039805659525644595)\\n\\nActive development warning. This project is being stress-tested by its author and is changing rapidly. Interfaces, config formats, and on-disk layouts may break between commits without notice. Not recommended for production use yet.\\n\\nCost warning. The daemon runs background agents (auditor, librarian, adversary, compliance reviewer) on configurable cron intervals that make real LLM calls against whatever API key you've configured. Before pointing this at a paid API (OpenRouter, OpenAI, etc.), review your ~/.llm-wiki/config.yaml maintenance intervals and set conservative values — or disable background workers you don't need. To cap spend, set cloud_hourly_limit and cloud_daily_limit in llm_queue config (weighted token units: input + output×5). Background maintenance calls are hard-blocked when a limit is reached; interactive query/ingest calls log a warning and proceed.\\n\\n## Why Not RAG? Why Not Plain Markdown?\\n#why-not-rag-why-not-plain-markdown\\n\\nRAG re-derives on every query. Your agent reads the same papers, synthesises the same conclusions, and starts from scratch next session. Nothing compounds. Every query is session zero.\\n\\nPlain markdown + a folder tool isn't much better. The agent dumps files into context (expensive, lossy), can't navigate a large vault efficiently, and produces a wiki that drifts into inconsistency with nothing maintaining it between sessions. Stale claims, broken wikilinks, orphaned pages — they accumulate, unchecked.\\n\\nLLM Wiki compiles knowledge into a graph that improves over time. Each ingested paper becomes pages with cross-references to existing content. Background agents keep claims honest against their sources. The wiki at month 6 is substantially richer than at day 1 — not just more pages, but better connections, fewer stale claims, and a usage-informed authority graph that knows which pages matter.\\n\\n## What You Get\\n#what-you-get\\n\\n### Compounding knowledge\\n#compounding-knowledge\\n\\nIngest a paper and it becomes wiki pages with wikilinks to existing content. Next ingest builds on those. Knowledge accumulates instead of evaporating. The cross-reference graph grows with every source.\\n\\n### Budget-aware traversal\\n#budget-aware-traversal\\n\\nAgents don't dump everything into context. They search, check a hierarchical manifest, read page headers and tables of contents, grep for specific terms, and follow wikilinks with intent — the same cognitive pattern as a human reading Wikipedia, at the same token efficiency. A 1000-page wiki doesn't cost 1000 pages of context.\\n\\n### Background quality agents\\n#background-quality-agents\\n\\nFour background workers run in t\n\n⚠️ [... middle content omitted — showing head and tail ...]\n\niki configure — it handles MCP registration for Hermes and Claude Code automatically. For manual setup:\\n\\nClaude Code — add to ~/.claude/mcp.json (or .claude/mcp.json in cwd for project-local):\\n\\n{\\n \\\"mcpServers\\\": {\\n \\\"llm-wiki\\\": {\\n \\\"command\\\": \\\"llm-wiki\\\",\\n \\\"args\\\": [\\\"mcp\\\"],\\n \\\"env\\\": { \\\"LLM_WIKI_VAULT\\\": \\\"/absolute/path/to/vault\\\" }\\n }\\n }\\n}\\n\\nHermes — add to ~/.hermes/config.yaml under mcp_servers::\\n\\n llm-wiki:\\n command: llm-wiki\\n args: [mcp]\\n env:\\n LLM_WIKI_VAULT: \\\"/absolute/path/to/vault\\\"\\n timeout: 120\\n\\nOther frameworks — any stdio MCP client: command llm-wiki mcp, env LLM_WIKI_VAULT.\\n\\nThe daemon auto-starts on first connect (allow ~30s on first call). 21 tools across five families:\\n\\nFamily\\nTools\\n\\nRead\\nwiki_search, wiki_read, wiki_manifest, wiki_status\\n\\nQuery\\nwiki_query, wiki_ingest, wiki_lint\\n\\nWrite\\nwiki_create, wiki_update, wiki_append\\n\\nMaintenance\\nwiki_issues_list/get/resolve, wiki_talk_read/post/list, wiki_session_close\\n\\nInbox\\nwiki_inbox_create, wiki_inbox_get, wiki_inbox_write, wiki_inbox_list\\n\\nEvery supervised write produces a git commit attributed to the calling agent via the Agent: trailer.\\n\\n## Agent Setup\\n#agent-setup\\n\\nFull setup walkthrough — vault creation, backend config, MCP registration, and patching any existing wiki skills — is in [skills/setup/SKILL.md](/Labhund/llm-wiki/blob/master/skills/setup/SKILL.md).\\n\\nExisting Obsidian vault? Set LLM_WIKI_VAULT to your vault root instead of creating a new directory. The daemon writes compiled pages to wiki/ (configurable), which Obsidian indexes alongside your existing notes.\\n\\n## Agent Skills\\n#agent-skills\\n\\nSkills that prime agents to use llm-wiki correctly by default — research traversal modes, citation discipline, conversational ingest, and maintenance hygiene. Skills are bundled with the pip package; llm-wiki configure installs them to your Hermes home automatically.\\n\\nAttended (user present): load skills/llm-wiki/ (or let the wizard install them)\\n\\nAutonomous (cron, swarm, unattended): load skills/llm-wiki/autonomous/<subskill> directly — different scope, conservative defaults, structured exit reports instead of check-ins.\\n\\nCompatible with Claude Code (via MCP tools) and Hermes (via skill files + MCP). Claude Code uses the MCP tool surface directly; no separate skill files needed.\\n\\n## How It Works\\n#how-it-works\\n\\nInterfaces CLI | MCP Server | Obsidian (direct file access)\\n |\\nDaemon | Unix socket IPC, file watcher, LLM queue,\\n | write coordinator, background workers\\n |\\nCore Library | Page parser, traversal engine, manifest store,\\n | search (tantivy), LLM abstraction (litellm)\\n |\\nStorage | Markdown files, tantivy index (~/.llm-wiki/),\\n | config, prompts\\n\\nWikipedia's governance model as agent roles: ingest agents write pages, the librarian improves cross-references from usage patterns, the adversary challenges claims against raw sources, the auditor checks structural integrity. Talk pages for async human-agent discussion.\\n\\n### Token Budgets\\n#token-budgets\\n\\nEvery operation is budget-aware:\\n\\n- Hierarchical manifest — cluster summaries → page entries → page content. Budget determines how deep you go.\\n\\n- Intra-page viewports — top (first section + TOC), section (by heading), grep (pattern match), full. Pages with %% section markers get precise slicing; plain headings fall back gracefully.\\n\\n- Pagination — search results, manifest entries, and viewport content all support cursor-based pagination within budget.\\n\\n### Section Markers\\n#section-markers\\n\\nObsidian-native hidden comments provide machine-readable section boundaries (invisible in Obsidian preview):\\n\\n%% section: overview, tokens: 120 %%\\n## Overview\\n\\nContent here...\\n\\n%% section: method, tokens: 280 %%\\n## Method\\n\\nContent here...\\n\\nNo markers? Falls back to ##/### heading-based slicing. No headings? Treated as one section.\\n\\n### Sessions and Com\\n<<<END_EXTERNAL_UNTRUSTED_CONTENT id=\\\"06117f04de42ec69\\\">>>\"\n}[... 5388 more characters truncated]"}],"details":{"persistedDetailsTruncated":true,"originalDetailsBytesAtLeast":8193,"originalDetailKeys":["url","finalUrl","status","contentType","title","extractMode","extractor","externalContent","truncated","length","rawLength","wrappedLength","fetchedAt","tookMs","text","warning"],"status":200,"truncated":true},"isError":false,"timestamp":1778048428316}}
